headline: Tech Layoffs Are Surpassing 2025 Levels. The Real Reason Companies Are Slashing Headcount.
summary: Tech companies keep announcing job cuts, and experts don’t expect that to stop anytime soon.  Reuters reported on Saturday that  Meta Platforms  is planning to conduct an initial wave of layoffs on May 20, with more coming later this year.  The report, citing sources familiar with the matter, said that Meta plans to lay off about 10% of its global workforce, or close to 8,000 employees, in that initial round.

headline: GitLab Collaborates with AWS to Bring Agentic DevSecOps to Enterprise Teams Using Their Existing Amazon Bedrock Accounts and Spend
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 21, 2026--All Remote - GitLab Inc., the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps, today announced a deepened integration with Amazon Web Services (AWS). Joint customers can now route GitLab Duo Agent Platform inference through Amazon Bedrock, powering agentic DevSecOps with the Amazon Bedrock models, IAM policies, and AWS spending commitments they already have in place.

headline: Microsoft must face $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences
summary: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ‌on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, ‌a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday.  Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf ​of nearly 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on rival cloud platforms.  They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

raw.headline: Microsoft must face $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences
raw.summary: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ‌on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, ‌a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday.  Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf ​of nearly 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on rival cloud platforms.  They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

headline: AI startup Anthropic commits $100 billion to Amazon's AWS over next 10 years
summary: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic has agreed to commit more than $100 billion to Amazon's AWS cloud platform over the next 10 years to train and run its Claude chatbot.  Amazon will invest $5 billion immediately as part of the new agreement announced this week by the companies, and up to another $20 billion in the future.  The partnership will allow Anthropic to secure up to 5 gigawatts of Amazon’s Trainium chips to train and power their artificial intelligence models.

raw.headline: AI startup Anthropic commits $100 billion to Amazon's AWS over next 10 years

Amazon will invest $5 billion immediately as part of the new agreement announced this week by the companies, and up to another $20 billion in the future.  The partnership will allow Anthropic to secure up to 5 gigawatts of Amazon’s Trainium chips to train and power their artificial intelligence models.

headline: Adobe CX Enterprise Pushes AI Agents Deeper Into Customer Experience Budgets
summary: Adobe, ticker NasdaqGS:ADBE, announced CX Enterprise, an agentic AI platform aimed at large businesses. The suite introduces business-focused AI agents for customer experience, digital marketing, content production, governance, and sales. Adobe is collaborating with Amazon, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and NVIDIA to support CX Enterprise across enterprise technology stacks.
